questions.q: In which Part of the Constitution of India are the Directive Principles of State Policy contained?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy are set out in Part IV of the Constitution.

questions.q: Are the Directive Principles of State Policy directly enforceable by the courts?
questions.explanation: Article 37 states that the Directive Principles are not enforceable by any court.

questions.q: From which country's constitution did India primarily draw the idea of Directive Principles?
questions.explanation: The concept of Directive Principles was borrowed mainly from the Constitution of Ireland.

questions.q: The Directive Principles are primarily intended to guide which entity in framing laws and policies?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles are guidelines addressed to the State for making laws and policies.

questions.q: The Directive Principles of State Policy chiefly aim to promote what kind of welfare?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles seek to establish social and economic welfare for the people.

questions.q: According to Article 37, the Directive Principles are described as what in the governance of the country?
questions.explanation: Article 37 declares that the principles are fundamental in the governance of the country.

questions.q: The Directive Principles of State Policy are often described as part of a constitutional concept known as the welfare what?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles embody the ideal of a welfare State.

questions.q: Which range of articles in the Constitution sets out the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles are contained in Articles 36 to 51 of Part IV.

questions.q: The Directive Principles of State Policy were part of the Constitution from which date?
questions.explanation: Part IV came into force when the Constitution commenced on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: Article 44 of the Directive Principles relates to securing for citizens which of the following?
questions.explanation: Article 44 directs the State to endeavour to secure a uniform civil code for citizens.

questions.q: Article 40 of the Constitution directs the State to organise which institution of self-government?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organise village panchayats as units of self-government.

questions.q: Which article among the Directive Principles directs the State to provide free and compulsory education for children, in its original form up to a certain age?
questions.explanation: Article 45 originally directed the State to provide free and compulsory education for children up to the age of fourteen.

questions.q: Article 48 of the Directive Principles concerns the organisation of agriculture and the prohibition of slaughter of which animals?
options: Cows and other milch and draught cattle
questions.explanation: Article 48 directs the State to prohibit the slaughter of cows, calves and other milch and draught cattle.

questions.q: Article 50 of the Directive Principles directs the State to take steps to separate which two branches?
questions.explanation: Article 50 directs the State to separate the judiciary from the executive in the public services.

questions.q: Article 39A, which directs the State to provide free legal aid, was added by which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: Article 39A on equal justice and free legal aid was inserted by the 42nd Amendment in 1976.

questions.q: Article 51 of the Directive Principles directs the State to promote which of the following?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the State to promote international peace and security and respect for international law.

questions.q: Article 48A, directing the State to protect and improve the environment and safeguard forests and wildlife, was inserted by which amendment?
questions.explanation: Article 48A on environmental protection was added by the 42nd Amendment in 1976.

questions.q: Which article among the Directive Principles directs the State to secure equal pay for equal work for both men and women?
questions.explanation: Article 39 includes the directive that there should be equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

questions.q: Article 47 of the Directive Principles relates to the State's duty to raise which of the following?
options: The strength of the armed forces
The level of nutrition and public health
The size of the civil service
questions.explanation: Article 47 directs the State to raise the level of nutrition, the standard of living and public health.

questions.q: Article 43 of the Directive Principles directs the State to secure for workers which of the following?
options: A living wage and decent conditions of work
questions.explanation: Article 43 directs the State to secure a living wage and decent conditions of work for all workers.

questions.q: Which provision directs the State to promote the educational and economic interests of the weaker sections, particularly Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes?
questions.explanation: Article 46 directs the State to promote the educational and economic interests of weaker sections, especially Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.

questions.q: Article 49 of the Directive Principles places a duty on the State to protect which of the following?
options: Monuments and objects of national importance
questions.explanation: Article 49 directs the State to protect monuments and places and objects of national importance.

questions.q: In the 1980 Minerva Mills case, the Supreme Court emphasised that there must be a balance and harmony between which two parts of the Constitution?
options: The Union List and the State List
questions.explanation: The Minerva Mills judgment held that the balance between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles is part of the basic structure.

questions.q: Article 45 was substituted by the 86th Amendment of 2002 to focus on the care and education of children of which age group?
options: Below the age of six years
Below the age of three years
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment recast Article 45 to provide for early childhood care and education for children below six years.

questions.q: Which Directive Principle directs the State to take steps to secure the participation of workers in the management of industries?
questions.explanation: Article 43A, added by the 42nd Amendment, directs the State to secure workers' participation in management of industries.

questions.q: Article 38(2), added by the 44th Amendment of 1978, directs the State to minimise inequalities in what?
questions.explanation: Article 38(2), inserted by the 44th Amendment, directs the State to minimise inequalities in income, status, facilities and opportunities.

questions.q: In the Constituent Assembly, the Directive Principles were sometimes described as a charter that would not be enforced by courts but by which of the following?
options: The electorate at the ballot box
questions.explanation: Members noted the principles would be enforced not by courts but ultimately through public opinion and the electorate.

questions.q: Article 39(b) and (c) deal with the distribution of which of the following to best subserve the common good?
options: The material resources of the community
questions.explanation: Article 39(b) and (c) direct that ownership and control of material resources be distributed to subserve the common good.

questions.q: Which Directive Principle directs the State to secure a social order in which justice, social, economic and political, informs national institutions?
questions.explanation: Article 38 directs the State to secure a social order promoting the welfare of the people through justice in all spheres.

questions.q: Article 41 of the Directive Principles directs the State, within its capacity, to secure the right to work, education and public assistance in cases of which of the following?
questions.explanation: Article 41 directs the State to provide public assistance in cases of unemployment, old age, sickness and disablement.
